track: capacity frontier — emb256 (above the emb128 peak)
probe: lightgcn_capacity_uniform_probe.py emb256 L4 reg1e-3 seed42, uniform split, 200ep

capacity_curve_uniform.note: single-seed uniform is flat from emb64->emb256 (~0.762); all capacity gain comes from seed ENSEMBLING, not dimensionality. emb256 single +0.0001 vs emb128 single = plateaued.

public_confirmation: emb192 (public 0.77715) already < emb128 (0.77745) despite higher uniform ens, and 8-seed (0.77625) < emb128 too. So scaling capacity/seeds beyond emb128 4-seed does NOT help public. emb256 would be even lower-value; NOT submitted (no quota waste on a known-lower candidate).
verdict: Capacity axis peaks at emb128. emb256 plateaus. emb128 4-seed ensemble (public 0.77745) remains the final #1. Capacity track CLOSED.
